prompt: Compare retrieval-augmented generation versus tool calling for a local assistant.

posts.author: @o3
posts.content: Tool calling is stronger when the workflow needs fresh external state.
posts.raw_content: Tool calling is stronger when the workflow needs fresh external state.

posts.author: @claude
posts.content: RAG is better for bounded corpora where latency matters more than actionability.
posts.raw_content: RAG is better for bounded corpora where latency matters more than actionability.

posts.author: @sonar
posts.content: A hybrid usually wins if retrieval stays grounded and tools stay narrow.
posts.raw_content: A hybrid usually wins if retrieval stays grounded and tools stay narrow.

posts.author: @o3
posts.content: Your latency point is right, but actions still need explicit tool boundaries.
posts.raw_content: Your latency point is right, but actions still need explicit tool boundaries.

posts.author: @o3
posts.content: I agree on hybridization, but only if the tool layer remains auditable.
posts.raw_content: I agree on hybridization, but only if the tool layer remains auditable.

posts.author: @claude
posts.content: Fresh state matters, although many assistant requests stay inside a trusted corpus.
posts.raw_content: Fresh state matters, although many assistant requests stay inside a trusted corpus.

posts.author: @claude
posts.content: Hybrid design works, but retrieval quality must be measured before tools compensate.
posts.raw_content: Hybrid design works, but retrieval quality must be measured before tools compensate.

posts.author: @sonar
posts.content: I agree on auditability, though retrieval can reduce needless tool invocations.
posts.raw_content: I agree on auditability, though retrieval can reduce needless tool invocations.

posts.author: @sonar
posts.content: Latency wins matter, but a hybrid lets retrieval and actions stay specialized.
posts.raw_content: Latency wins matter, but a hybrid lets retrieval and actions stay specialized.

posts.author: @sonar
posts.content: The council favored a hybrid design with explicit tooling and grounded retrieval.
posts.raw_content: The council favored a hybrid design with explicit tooling and grounded retrieval.

points_of_agreement: A hybrid architecture is preferable for the target assistant.
points_of_disagreement: They disagree on how much retrieval can replace tools.
unresolved_questions: Which evaluation mix best captures latency and task success?
minutes.recommended_next_action: Benchmark a hybrid pipeline with fixed prompts.
minutes.raw_minutes_text: The council favored a hybrid design with explicit tooling and grounded retrieval.
